steps.title: Make two cost points
steps.content: Use the first valid anchor point and one extra hour. The first 4 hours cost 250 dollars, so the line passes through $(4,250)$. One more hour adds 55 dollars, so 5 hours cost 305 dollars, giving the point $(5,305)$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: The slope is $$m=\frac{305-250}{5-4}=55.$$ Substitute $(4,250)$ into $y=55x+b$: $$250=55(4)+b,$$ so $b=30$. The total charge is $$y=55x+30.$$ The $30$ is not a real starting charge at zero hours; it is the intercept that makes the line pass through the 4-hour flat-rate point.

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice C — $y = 55x + 30$ is $y=55x+30$. It gives $y=250$ when $x=4$ and increases by 55 for each additional hour. Choice A — $y = 55x + 250$ double-counts the flat 250 dollars, while the expressions using 250 as the hourly rate use the wrong slope. That matches Choice C — $y = 55x + 30$.
